Most agents are easy to install. It is an \u003cstrong\u003eecosystem configurator\u003c/strong\u003e -- it takes whatever AI coding agent(s) you use and supercharges them with persistent memory, Spec-Driven Development workflows, curated coding skills, MCP servers, an AI provider switcher, a teaching-oriented persona with security-first permissions, and per-phase model assignment so each SDD step can run on a different model.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eBefore\u003c/strong\u003e: \"I installed Claude Code / OpenCode / Cursor, but it's just a chatbot that writes Once a task stops being small, delegation or an explicit SDD phase boundary is expected rather than optional.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eTrigger\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eExpected behavior\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eReading 4+ files to understand a flow\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eDelegate exploration or run an exploration phase.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eTouching 2+ non-trivial files\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eUse one writer or require fresh review before completion.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eCommit, push, or PR after code changes\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eRun fresh review unless the diff is trivial docs/text.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eWrong cwd, worktree/git accident, merge recovery, confusing test/env issue\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eStop and run a fresh audit before continuing.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eLong monolithic session with accumulating complexity\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003ePause and delegate, re-plan, or justify why not.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAdversarial review of diffs, conflicts, PR readiness, or incidents\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eUse fresh context when the agent platform supports it.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe goal is not ceremony. The goal is to avoid accidental chaos while preserving one responsible orchestrator and one writer thread.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" register the project context:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eCommand\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eWhat it does\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eWhen to re-run\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/sdd-init\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eDetects stack, testing capabilities, activates Strict TDD Mode if available\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eWhen your project adds/removes test frameworks, or first time in a new project\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003egentle-ai skill-registry refresh\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eScans installed skills and project conventions, builds the registry\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAfter installing/removing skills, or first time in a new project\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThese are \u003cstrong\u003enot required\u003c/strong\u003e for basic usage. The SDD orchestrator runs \u003ccode\u003e/sdd-init\u003c/code\u003e automatically if it detects no context. Startup hooks normally keep the skill registry fresh for agents that support hooks, including Pi through \u003ccode\u003egentle-pi\u003c/code\u003e. If you start Pi with \u003ccode\u003epi -ns\u003c/code\u003e, startup skill loading/hooks are skipped, so run the registry refresh manually when you need updated project rules.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eRun \u003ccode\u003egentle-ai doctor\u003c/code\u003e at any time for a read-only health check of your ecosystem (tool binaries, \u003ccode\u003estate.json\u003c/code\u003e, Engram reachability, disk space).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" To keep the Gentleman stack isolated to one project, run:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"gentle-ai install --scope=workspace\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003egentle-ai install --scope=workspace\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWorkspace scope is not Claude-only; it applies to selected agents for agent-scoped files such as system prompts, skills, SDD agents, and persona files. Global-only integrations remain global by design.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eBackups\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-backups\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Backups\" href=\"#backups\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eEvery install, sync, and upgrade automatically snapshots your config files. Backups are \u003cstrong\u003ecompressed\u003c/strong\u003e (tar.gz), \u003cstrong\u003ededuplicated\u003c/strong\u003e (identical configs are not re-backed up), and \u003cstrong\u003eauto-pruned\u003c/strong\u003e (keeps the 5 most recent). Pin important backups via the TUI (\u003ccode\u003ep\u003c/code\u003e key) to protect them from pruning.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eSee \u003ca TUI plugin to manage SDD profiles and browse Engram memories directly from OpenCode, with runtime profile activation and no restart required.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhen you select OpenCode in the installer, Gentle-AI asks whether to register each community plugin and offers a browser shortcut to review the repository first. Gentle-AI only ensures \u003ccode\u003e~/.config/opencode/tui.json\u003c/code\u003e exists and adds the plugin package names to its \u003ccode\u003eplugin\u003c/code\u003e array; OpenCode installs/loads those packages the next time it starts. Once OpenCode has materialized a plugin under \u003ccode\u003e~/.config/opencode/node_modules/\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003egentle-ai update\u003c/code\u003e can compare its local \u003ccode\u003epackage.json\u003c/code\u003e version with the plugin's GitHub releases.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eContributors\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-contributors\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Contributors\" href=\"#contributors\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis project exists because of the community. class="react-directory-row-commit-cell"><div><div class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="feat(uninstall): add granular uninstall flow on current main (#241)

* fix(uninstall): harden managed cleanup and atomic writes

* fix(uninstall): address second audit findings

* docs(usage): document granular uninstall flow

* feat(tui): add uninstall mode selector with workspace asset warnings

- Add ScreenUninstallMode as entry point for uninstall flow
- Three modes: Partial (granular selection), Full (all agents/components), Full & Remove (includes binary deletion)
- Workspace asset warnings for .engram/, workflows/, and skills/
- Full & Remove mode uses os.Remove() to delete gentle-ai binary after successful uninstall
- Updated tests to reflect new navigation flow through mode selector
- Closes part of #102

* feat(uninstall): report manual cleanup required items

- Extend uninstall result with ManualActions
- Report directories left non-empty after managed cleanup
- Show manual cleanup section in CLI and TUI uninstall results
- Add tests for service, CLI report, and TUI result rendering

* fix(uninstall): resolve Copilot review feedback on TUI and service logic

* feat(uninstall): add manual CLI command cleanup instruction on full uninstall

* feat(uninstall): add 'Full Uninstall + Clean Install' mode

Adds a 4th uninstall mode that removes all managed configuration and
immediately re-syncs all managed assets from scratch. This provides
a factory-reset workflow to fix broken configurations without manually
re-running install/sync commands.

The sync runs after uninstall completes. Sync errors are non-fatal and
displayed separately in the result screen so the user always sees the
uninstall status.

* fix(uninstall): clean SDD profiles during removal

* fix(uninstall): wire TUI uninstall path and onboard cleanup

Ensure managed uninstall is reachable from the Welcome menu and correctly wired to the backend callback, and include sdd-onboard in SDD cleanup lists to avoid leftover managed artifacts.

* fix(uninstall): resolve review regressions in full-remove and atomic writes

Restore WriteFileAtomic permission-relaxation compatibility, prevent Homebrew full-remove from deleting managed binaries, and tighten uninstall cleanup safety checks so reviewer-reported edge cases are handled explicitly.

* feat(uninstall): add profile sub-selection inside uninstall flow

Add a conditional profile-selection step so users can choose which OpenCode SDD profiles are removed, while preserving the old flow when no profiles apply.

* feat(uninstall): add Engram project-vs-global cleanup scope

* fix: resolve rebase conflicts with main after PR #265 state.Write API change

- Adapt uninstall service to use state.InstallState instead of []string
- Update app.go dispatch to match RunUninstall return signature
- Replace incompatible uninstall tests with PR-compatible version

---------

title="fix(persona): remove argentinisms from shared assets leaking voseo into neutral mode

Shared infrastructure assets (engram protocol, SDD orchestrator, Windsurf
workflows) contained Argentine Spanish expressions that caused LLMs to infer
voseo context and respond with 'vos' even in neutral persona mode.

Replacements:
- acordate → removed (recordar already present as neutral form)
- ¿Seguimos? → ¿Continuamos? (all 7 sdd-orchestrator variants)
- dale/Dale → de acuerdo (windsurf workflow + orchestrator approval gates)

Gentleman persona files are intentionally untouched — they retain all
Rioplatense expressions as designed." class="Link--secondary" href="/Gentleman-Programming/gentle-ai/commit/9f5b6d80187265f32c8f69e22c4b2d5eddee148b">fix(persona): remove argentinisms from shared title="feat(update): self-update on startup with BuildInfo version fallback

Check for newer gentle-ai releases at startup and auto-apply before
CLI/TUI dispatch. Re-execs with same args on Unix; prints restart
message on Windows. All errors are non-fatal.

Also fixes version detection for go-install users: falls back to
debug.BuildInfo when ldflags version is "dev", so only true dev
builds (git clone + go build) are skipped.

- Add ResolveVersion() with debug.BuildInfo fallback
- Add selfUpdate() with guard chain (done/opt-out/dev)
- 7s network timeout, GENTLE_AI_NO_SELF_UPDATE=1 opt-out
- 16 new tests covering all paths including Windows and brew" class="Link--secondary" href="/Gentleman-Programming/gentle-ai/commit/bbaa4e103c25e0f1f25ed41c84538fec2043c16c">feat(update): self-update minimal preset tests from --persona neutral to --persona custom (#673)

* fix(tui): decouple persona from preset and rename preset labels

ComponentPersona is now driven by Selection.Persona (single source of
truth) rather than by the preset's component list. Persona screen changes
recompute components when a non-custom preset is already set. Preset labels
are renamed to outcome-based names (Memory Only, Dev Stack, Dev Stack +
Polish) while internal PresetID constants remain unchanged for backward
compatibility with state.json and --preset CLI flag.

Closes #670

* test(e2e): update preset tests for decoupled persona

After decoupling persona from preset, persona is appended to the
component list whenever Selection.Persona != Custom. The minimal and
ecosystem preset tests previously asserted persona was excluded — that
held only because the preset itself excluded ComponentPersona. With the
decouple, persona inclusion is driven by the persona flag.

Updates:
- test_preset_minimal_components: pass --persona custom to keep the negative assertion
- test_preset_ecosystem_components: same, --persona custom
- New test_preset_minimal_with_default_persona_includes_persona: covers default persona path
- New test_preset_full_with_custom_persona_excludes_persona: covers the inverse case

* test(e2e): switch minimal preset tests from --persona neutral to --persona custom

The intent of these tests was 'minimal install has no persona side effect'.
Before the persona/preset decouple, that worked because PresetMinimal's
component list excluded ComponentPersona regardless of which persona the
user picked. After the decouple, picking --persona neutral now installs
the neutral persona files (correct new behavior). Switching to
--persona custom keeps the original intent: persona is skipped." class="Link--secondary" href="/Gentleman-Programming/gentle-ai/commit/96fded7ebbbaa05fb28ee41acf04e595ee4d3847">fix(e2e): switch class="react-directory-row-commit-cell"><div><div class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="fix(install): persist PATH, fix UTF-8 encoding, and harden verify step on Windows (#711)

* fix(install): fix PATH persistence, UTF-8 encoding, and verify step on Windows

- Persist the install directory to the User PATH via SetEnvironmentVariable so
  the binary is reachable in new shells after install (not just warned about).
- Add chcp 65001 + [Console]::OutputEncoding = UTF8 preamble so Unicode glyphs
  render correctly on legacy Windows codepages instead of garbling as Ôáï/Ô£ù.
- Rewrite Test-Installation to probe the binary by absolute path using --version
  with GENTLE_AI_NO_SELF_UPDATE=1, preventing the verify step from triggering
  self-update logic or resolving a stale binary via the old PATH.

Closes #201

* fix(install): harden PowerShell script against Windows edge-case footguns

- Wrap Console::OutputEncoding setter in try/catch to prevent abort in
  non-console hosts (ISE, CI, remoting) under ErrorActionPreference=Stop
- Add REG_EXPAND_SZ flattening caveat comment on GetEnvironmentVariable
- Replace wildcard substring PATH check with exact case-insensitive
  split-and-compare to avoid false positives and wildcard injection
- Replace '$env:VAR = $null' with Remove-Item Env:VAR -ErrorAction
  SilentlyContinue so the variable is actually removed, not left empty" class="Link--secondary" href="/Gentleman-Programming/gentle-ai/commit/1656b55541266b941c16c69a5186c951021e6ac4">fix(install): implementation of AI Gentle Stack

Go CLI installer that configures the Gentleman AI ecosystem into
AI coding agents (Claude Code, OpenCode) across macOS and Linux.

Core features:
- Platform detection with profile-aware command resolution (brew/apt/pacman)
- Dependency-first planning with topological ordering
- Staged pipeline execution with backup/rollback
- Component injectors: Engram, SDD, Skills, MCP, Persona, Permissions, GGA
- Agent adapters with capability interface for extensibility
- Post-apply verification and readiness reporting
- Non-interactive CLI with dry-run support
- Bubbletea TUI with Rose Pine theme
- Docker-based E2E tests for Ubuntu and Arch Linux" class="Link--secondary" href="/Gentleman-Programming/gentle-ai/commit/4c9176e0b4d53a4105886f030b9bce5da45fb478">feat: initial implementation of AI JD agent definitions and model assignment foundation (#475)

* feat: add 3 JD agent definitions to sdd-overlay-multi.json

Add jd-judge-a, jd-judge-b, and jd-fix-agent as hidden sub-agents
in the multi-mode overlay. Update orchestrator permissions to allow
delegation to these agents.

Refs: #208

* feat: add JDPhases() and ConfigurableAgentPhases() to opencode

JDPhases returns the judgment-day sub-agent names (jd-judge-a,
jd-judge-b, jd-fix-agent). ConfigurableAgentPhases combines SDD
phases and JD agents for the TUI model picker.

Refs: #208

* feat: add JD agents to model assignment tables and presets

Add jd-judge-a, jd-judge-b, jd-fix-agent to claudeModelAssignmentRowOrder
and claudeModelAssignmentReasons in inject.go. Add JD entries to all three
existing presets (balanced, performance, economy) in claude_model.go.
Add new ClaudeModelPresetDiversity() preset for perspective diversity
between judges (opus/haiku/sonnet split).

Refs: #208

* feat: exclude JD agents from root model propagation

Add isJDAgent helper and modify injectModelAssignments case 3 to skip
root model fallback for judgment-day agents. This preserves independent
model configuration for diversity of perspective between judges.

Refs: #208

* fix: clarify ConfigurableAgentPhases docstring — TUI uses SDDPhases/JDPhases separately

* fix: use maps.Clone and set lookup to prevent mutation bugs

- ClaudeModelPresetDiversity now clones the base map before mutating,
  preventing corruption if ClaudeModelPresetBalanced is ever cached.
- isJDAgent uses a package-level set instead of linear scan, consistent
  with sddPhaseSet pattern in read_assignments.go.

* fix: remove unsupported glob/grep tool keys from JD judge agents

OpenCode overlays only use {read, write, edit, bash} as tool keys.
glob and grep are not referenced anywhere in the codebase and may cause
config rejection. Judges only need read and bash for code review.

* fix: update tests for JD agent entries in overlay and presets

- Regenerate golden file (sdd-opencode-multi-settings.golden) to include
  jd-judge-a, jd-judge-b, and jd-fix-agent entries
- Update TestInjectOpenCodeMultiMode agent count: 11 → 14
  (gentle-orchestrator + 10 SDD phases + 3 JD agents)
- Update TestDefaultOverlayTaskPermissions_ExplicitAllowlist to expect
  JD agent permissions in the multi overlay only
- Update TUI model picker tests to expect orchestrator in balanced
  preset (now a configurable assignment in the model table)

* chore: add .pi-lens/ to .gitignore and untrack cache artifacts

---------

title="feat(agent-builder): add TUI flow for creating custom AI sub-agents (#223)

Add a 'Create your own Agent' option to the Welcome screen that guides
users through a 6-step flow: choose an AI engine (Claude/OpenCode/Gemini/
Codex), describe the desired agent in natural language, configure optional
SDD integration (standalone, phase support, or new phase), generate a
SKILL.md via the selected engine CLI, preview the result, and install it
across all configured agents.

New internal/agentbuilder package with GenerationEngine interface, prompt
composer, output parser with validation, atomic cross-agent installer,
JSON registry at ~/.config/gentle-ai/custom-agents.json, and SDD marker
injection. 8 new TUI screens following existing Bubbletea patterns.

Includes ~103 new tests: unit (parser, prompt, registry, installer, sdd,
engine), screen render smoke tests, TUI navigation flow tests, and an
end-to-end integration test with MockEngine.

Closes #222" class="Link--secondary" href="/Gentleman-Programming/gentle-ai/commit/8a54d9b6972045de0f47aa66f3898f47085ea0af">feat(agent-builder): add TUI flow for creating custom AI sub-agents (</a><a <div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h1 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Gentle-AI</h1><a id="user-content-gentle-ai" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Gentle-AI" href="#gentle-ai"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Gentle-AI — Ecosystem, Frameworks, Workflows for AI coding agents.</strong></p>
<p dir="auto">
<a href="https://github.com/Gentleman-Programming/gentle-ai/releases"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/cd76fd4b80d5ea7fc0110e6ecafbfed9bce7057b9bc66251e7ee0d111941f897/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f762f72656c656173652f47656e746c656d616e2d50726f6772616d6d696e672f67656e746c652d6169" alt="Release" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/Gentleman-Programming/gentle-ai" style="max-width: 100%;"></a>

</p>
</div>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">What It Does</h2><a id="user-content-what-it-does" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: What It Does" href="#what-it-does"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Gentle-AI is NOT an AI agent installer. Most agents are easy to install. It is an <strong>ecosystem configurator</strong> -- it takes whatever AI coding agent(s) you use and supercharges them with persistent memory, Spec-Driven Development workflows, curated coding skills, MCP servers, an AI provider switcher, a teaching-oriented persona with security-first permissions, and per-phase model assignment so each SDD step can run on a different model.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Before</strong>: "I installed Claude Code / OpenCode / Cursor, but it's just a chatbot that writes code."</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>After</strong>: Your agent now has memory, skills, workflow, MCP tools, and a persona that actually teaches you.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">15 Supported Agents</h3><a id="user-content-15-supported-agents" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 15 Supported Agents" href="#15-supported-agents"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>

<tr>

<blockquote>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Note</strong>: This project supersedes <a href="https://github.com/Gentleman-Programming/agent-teams-lite">Agent Teams Lite</a> (now archived). Everything ATL provided is included here with better installation, automatic updates, and persistent memory.</p>
</blockquote>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Delegation Triggers</h3><a id="user-content-delegation-triggers" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Delegation Triggers" href="#delegation-triggers"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Gentle-AI keeps the parent/orchestrator thread thin. Once a task stops being small, delegation or an explicit SDD phase boundary is expected rather than optional.</p>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Trigger</th>
<th>Expected behavior</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Reading 4+ files to understand a flow</td>
<td>Delegate exploration or run an exploration phase.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Touching 2+ non-trivial files</td>
<td>Use one writer or require fresh review before completion.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Commit, push, or PR after code changes</td>
<td>Run fresh review unless the diff is trivial docs/text.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Wrong cwd, worktree/git accident, merge recovery, confusing test/env issue</td>
<td>Stop and run a fresh audit before continuing.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Long monolithic session with accumulating complexity</td>
<td>Pause and delegate, re-plan, or justify why not.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Adversarial review of diffs, conflicts, PR readiness, or incidents</td>
<td>Use fresh context when the agent platform supports it.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>
<p dir="auto">The goal is not ceremony. The goal is to avoid accidental chaos while preserving one responsible orchestrator and one writer thread.</p>
<hr>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">After install: project-level setup</h3><a id="user-content-after-install-project-level-setup" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: After install: project-level setup" href="#after-install-project-level-setup"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Once your agents are configured, open your AI agent in a project and run these two commands to register the project context:</p>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Command</th>
<th>What it does</th>
<th>When to re-run</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>/sdd-init</code></td>
<td>Detects stack, testing capabilities, activates Strict TDD Mode if available</td>
<td>When your project adds/removes test frameworks, or first time in a new project</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>gentle-ai skill-registry refresh</code></td>
<td>Scans installed skills and project conventions, builds the registry</td>
<td>After installing/removing skills, or first time in a new project</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>
<p dir="auto">These are <strong>not required</strong> for basic usage. The SDD orchestrator runs <code>/sdd-init</code> automatically if it detects no context. Startup hooks normally keep the skill registry fresh for agents that support hooks, including Pi through <code>gentle-pi</code>. If you start Pi with <code>pi -ns</code>, startup skill loading/hooks are skipped, so run the registry refresh manually when you need updated project rules.</p>
<p dir="auto">Run <code>gentle-ai doctor</code> at any time for a read-only health check of your ecosystem (tool binaries, <code>state.json</code>, Engram reachability, disk space).</p>
<hr>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h4 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Windows</h4><a id="user-content-windows-1" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Windows" href="#windows-1"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Use Scoop on Windows. It is the supported install path for keeping Gentle AI updated cleanly:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-powershell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>scoop bucket add gentleman https:<span class="pl-k">//</span><span class="pl-c1">github.com</span><span class="pl-k">/</span>Gentleman<span class="pl-k">-</span>Programming<span class="pl-k">/</span>scoop<span class="pl-k">-</span>bucket
scoop install gentle<span class="pl-k">-</span>ai</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="scoop bucket add gentleman https://github.com/Gentleman-Programming/scoop-bucket
scoop install gentle-ai" tabindex="0" role="button">
      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">

<p dir="auto">By default, <code>gentle-ai install</code> writes agent-scoped files to each selected agent's global config directory. To keep the Gentleman stack isolated to one project, run:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>gentle-ai install --scope=workspace</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="gentle-ai install --scope=workspace" tabindex="0" role="button">
      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">
    <path d="M0 6.75C0 5.784.784 5 1.75 5h1.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-1.5a.25.25 0 0 0-.25.25v7.5c0 .138.112.25.25.25h7.5a.25.25 0 0 0 .25-.25v-1.5a.75.75 0 0 1 1.5 0v1.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 9.25 16h-7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 0 14.25Z"></path><path d="M5 1.75C5 .784 5.784 0 6.75 0h7.5C15.216 0 16 .784 16 1.75v7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 14.25 11h-7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 5 9.25Zm1.75-.25a.25.25 0 0 0-.25.25v7.5c0 .138.112.25.25.25h7.5a.25.25 0 0 0 .25-.25v-7.5a.25.25 0 0 0-.25-.25Z"></path>
</svg>
      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-check js-clipboard-check-icon color-fg-success d-none">
    <path d="M13.78 4.22a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.06l-7.25 7.25a.75.75 0 0 1-1.06 0L2.22 9.28a.751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018L6 10.94l6.72-6.72a.75.75 0 0 1 1.06 0Z"></path>
</svg>
    </clipboard-copy>
  </div></div>
<p dir="auto">Workspace scope is not Claude-only; it applies to selected agents for agent-scoped files such as system prompts, skills, SDD agents, and persona files. Global-only integrations remain global by design.</p>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Backups</h2><a id="user-content-backups" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Backups" href="#backups"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Every install, sync, and upgrade automatically snapshots your config files. Backups are <strong>compressed</strong> (tar.gz), <strong>deduplicated</strong> (identical configs are not re-backed up), and <strong>auto-pruned</strong> (keeps the 5 most recent). Pin important backups via the TUI (<code>p</code> key) to protect them from pruning.</p>
<p dir="auto">See <a href="/Gentleman-Programming/gentle-ai/blob/main/docs/rollback.md">Backup & Rollback Guide</a> for details.</p>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Key Features You Should Know About</h2><a id="user-content-key-features-you-should-know-about" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Key Features You Should Know About" href="#key-features-you-should-know-about"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">OpenCode SDD Profiles</h3><a id="user-content-opencode-sdd-profiles" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: OpenCode SDD Profiles" href="#opencode-sdd-profiles"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Assign different AI models to different SDD phases -- a powerful model for design, a fast one for implementation, a cheap one for exploration. OpenCode uses <strong><code>gentle-orchestrator</code></strong> as the base SDD conductor, and generated named profiles still appear as <code>sdd-orchestrator-{name}</code> entries.</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Via CLI</span>
gentle-ai sync --profile cheap:openrouter/qwen/qwen3-30b-a3b:free
gentle-ai sync --profile-phase cheap:sdd-design:anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Or via TUI: gentle-ai → "OpenCode SDD Profiles" → Create</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="# Via CLI
gentle-ai sync --profile cheap:openrouter/qwen/qwen3-30b-a3b:free
gentle-ai sync --profile-phase cheap:sdd-design:anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514

# Or via TUI: gentle-ai → "OpenCode SDD Profiles" → Create" tabindex="0" role="button">
      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">

      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-check js-clipboard-check-icon color-fg-success d-none">
    <path d="M13.78 4.22a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.06l-7.25 7.25a.75.75 0 0 1-1.06 0L2.22 9.28a.751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018L6 10.94l6.72-6.72a.75.75 0 0 1 1.06 0Z"></path>

<p dir="auto">After creating a profile, open OpenCode and press <strong>Tab</strong> to switch between <code>gentle-orchestrator</code> (default) and your custom profiles.</p>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>What you need</th>
<th>Use this</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Default SDD conductor</td>
<td><code>gentle-orchestrator</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Legacy configs</td>
<td><code>sdd-orchestrator</code> is migrated to <code>gentle-orchestrator</code> on sync</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Named model profiles</td>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Engram (Persistent Memory)</h3><a id="user-content-engram-persistent-memory" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Engram (Persistent Memory)" href="#engram-persistent-memory"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Your AI agent automatically remembers decisions, bugs, and context across sessions. You don't need to do anything -- but when you do:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>engram projects list          <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> See all projects with memory counts</span>
engram projects consolidate   <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Fix name drift ("my-app" vs "My-App")</span>
engram search <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>auth bug<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>      <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Find a past decision from the terminal</span>
engram tui                    <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Visual memory browser</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="engram projects list          # See all projects with memory counts
engram projects consolidate   # Fix name drift ("my-app" vs "My-App")
engram search "auth bug"      # Find a past decision from the terminal
engram tui                    # Visual memory browser" tabindex="0" role="button">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Community Highlights</h2><a id="user-content-community-highlights" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Community Highlights" href="#community-highlights"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">This project gets better when the community builds on top of it.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Community Integrations</h3><a id="user-content-community-integrations" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Community Integrations" href="#community-integrations"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://github.com/Joaquinvesapa/sub-agent-statusline">sub-agent-statusline</a> — optional OpenCode TUI plugin that shows sub-agent activity, status, elapsed time, and token/context usage when OpenCode exposes it.</li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/j0k3r-dev-rgl/sdd-engram-plugin">sdd-engram-plugin</a> — optional OpenCode TUI plugin to manage SDD profiles and browse Engram memories directly from OpenCode, with runtime profile activation and no restart required.</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">When you select OpenCode in the installer, Gentle-AI asks whether to register each community plugin and offers a browser shortcut to review the repository first. Gentle-AI only ensures <code>~/.config/opencode/tui.json</code> exists and adds the plugin package names to its <code>plugin</code> array; OpenCode installs/loads those packages the next time it starts. Once OpenCode has materialized a plugin under <code>~/.config/opencode/node_modules/</code>, <code>gentle-ai update</code> can compare its local <code>package.json</code> version with the plugin's GitHub releases.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Contributors</h2><a id="user-content-contributors" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Contributors" href="#contributors"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">This project exists because of the community. See <a href="/Gentleman-Programming/gentle-ai/blob/main/CONTRIBUTORS.md">CONTRIBUTORS.md</a> for the full list.</p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Next Steps</h2><a id="user-content-next-steps" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Next Steps" href="#next-steps"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Just installed?</strong> Read <a href="/Gentleman-Programming/gentle-ai/blob/main/docs/intended-usage.md">Intended Usage</a> -- the one page that explains the mental model.</li>
<li><strong>Using OpenCode?</strong> Set up <a href="/Gentleman-Programming/gentle-ai/blob/main/docs/opencode-profiles.md">SDD Profiles</a> to assign different models per phase.</li>
<li><strong>Using Pi?</strong> Read <a href="/Gentleman-Programming/gentle-ai/blob/main/docs/pi.md">Pi Agent</a> for Pi commands, persona, model assignments, and package behavior.</li>
<li><strong>Want to share memory across machines?</strong> Learn <code>engram sync</code> in the <a href="/Gentleman-Programming/gentle-ai/blob/main/docs/engram.md">Engram reference</a>.</li>
<li><strong>Ready to contribute?</strong> Check <a href="/Gentleman-Programming/gentle-ai/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md">CONTRIBUTING.md</a> and the <a href="https://github.com/Gentleman-Programming/gentle-ai/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen+label%3A%22status%3Aapproved%22">open issues</a>.</li>
</ul>
